title: Bridge Essay: Psychosis and urbanicity: a review of the recent literature from epidemiology to neurourbanism.
concept: Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.

**Psychosis and urbanicity: a review of the recent literature from epidemiology to neurourbanism.** - Curr Opin Psychiatry (2019). DOI: 10.1097/YCO.0000000000000486. PMID: 30724751. PURPOSE OF REVIEW: Epidemiological studies associate city living with an elevated psychosis risk. Urban (social/economic) stress and exposure to environmental toxins, pollution or disease agents have been proposed to underlie this association. This review provides an update on the recent evidence (May 2017 - November 2018).
